39c5a9188796f8f241acd68abb669be6a33d2fab: Regarding the European Parliament plenary meeting's adoption of the "Policy Recommendations on the Changing Geopolitical Situation in East Asia and the Need to Strengthen Cooperation with Like-minded Partners in the Region" report, which recommends that the EU deepens cooperation with democratic partners such as Japan, South Korea and Taiwan, reaffirms the importance of maintaining peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, and opposes any unilateral change of the status quo through coercion, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs responded as follows:
7a5325c4c2bb76b4f886333aae3282da233e3e11: 2026/07/09 The European Parliament adopted the "Recommendation on the changing geopolitical situation in East Asia and the need for closer cooperation with like-minded partners in the region" on July 7 this year (2026). region), recommends that the EU deepens cooperation with democratic partners such as Japan, South Korea and Taiwan, reiterates the importance of maintaining peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, and opposes any act of unilaterally changing the status quo through coercion. Foreign Minister Lin Jialong expressed his sincere welcome and gratitude to the European Parliament for its continued concrete actions to support Taiwan. The report pointed out that Taiwan is a reliable and key democratic partner of the EU in East Asia. The peace and stability of the Taiwan Strait and the South China Sea are of great strategic interest to the EU and are also crucial to global security and prosperity. It is recommended that the EU should deepen cooperation with partners with similar concepts such as Japan, South Korea, Taiwan and ASEAN countries. Taiwan The EU can establish a comprehensive and structural cooperation framework based on operational mechanisms such as the "Trade and Investment Dialogue"; at the same time, it supports Taiwan's meaningful participation in international organizations and technical forums, respects freedom of navigation and overflight in the South China Sea and the Taiwan Strait, and encourages members of the European Parliament and our country's Legislative Yuan to deepen congressional diplomacy. This case once again shows that the European Parliament attaches great importance to peace and stability in Taiwan and the Taiwan Strait. It is also a concrete action to express its firm support for Taiwan after the 2023 "Taiwan-EU Trade and Investment Relations", the 2024 "The People's Republic of China's Improper Interpretation of United Nations General Assembly Resolution 2758 and Continued Military Provocations against Taiwan", and the European Parliament's "Common Foreign and Security Policy (CFSP) and "Common Security and Defense Policy" (CSDP) 2025 Annual Implementation Report Resolutions. Taiwan will continue to strengthen cooperation with the EU and partners with similar ideas, work together to maintain peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait and the Indo-Pacific region, jointly defend the rules-based international order, and actively make more contributions to global peace, security, and prosperity.
980428c3ec3b5d743ba3045c63a15913faf0a268: Foreign Minister Lin Jialong receives a delegation from the "Transnational Inter-Parliamentary Alliance on China Policy"
a800d20a1e1d49514dff6532a196a0c9401ae9e9: 2026/07/09 Press Release No. 313 Foreign Minister Lin Jialong received an 8-member delegation from the "Inter-Parliamentary Alliance on China Policy" (IPAC) on the afternoon of July 7 this year (2026). Parliamentary Secretary Wu Zhizhong hosted a banquet on behalf of the delegation in the evening of the same day to express the sincere welcome of our government. Members of the visiting delegation include Ukrainian MP Yulia Sirko, British MP Alicia Kearns, Dutch MP Jan Paternotte, New Zealand MP Helen White, former Indian MP Priyanka Chaturvedi, Czech MP Katerina Demetrashvili, British MP Tom Tugendhat) and Luke de Pulford, founder and executive director of IPAC, etc. Minister Lin first welcomed the IPAC delegation's visit to Taiwan and thanked IPAC for its long-term and steadfast support to Taiwan, including inviting Vice President Hsiao Meiqin to attend the European Parliament in November last year to attend the IPAC Annual Meeting in Brussels to deliver a speech. In April this year, he issued a statement condemning Beijing for putting its political will above other countries' airspace sovereignty and supporting President Lai Ching-te to communicate freely with international partners without coercion or interference. Minister Lin said that Taiwan will continue to strengthen self-defense and the resilience of society as a whole, and work with IPAC to defend common values ​​such as democracy, freedom, and human rights. Representative Hiccup responded that the visiting delegation members come from different countries and all face challenges brought by authoritarianism. IPAC will firmly support Taiwan's democracy and self-determination rights and firmly believes that only by being fully prepared can real peace be maintained. Political Undersecretary Wu said at the dinner that China continues to expand its authoritarian influence and intrudes on Taiwan with gray zone operations and military intimidation, seriously undermining regional peace and stability. The international community has deeply understood that peace in the Taiwan Strait is inseparable from the interests of all countries, and looks forward to IPAC and partners with similar concepts to continue to deepen cooperation with Taiwan. Former Senator Kaduviti said that the visiting delegation visited Chinese government agencies and civil society organizations and witnessed first-hand the vigorous development of Taiwan's democracy. IPAC will continue to gather transnational members of Congress with similar ideas to jointly defend universal values ​​such as democracy, human rights, and freedom. IPAC was established in June 2020. It is composed of cross-party elected members of parliaments and European Parliaments around the world. Currently, more than 300 members of parliaments and European Parliaments from 44 countries have participated. IPAC is an important transnational parliamentary organization paying attention to the challenges China poses to democratic countries. It is committed to promoting legislation in various countries to prevent China's political and economic penetration and coercion, and has deep international influence. During their stay in Taiwan, in addition to attending the "2026 Taiwan International Ocean Forum" hosted by the Ocean Commission, the delegation jointly met with President Lai Ching-te and Vice President Hsiao Meiqin, received a banquet hosted by National Security Council Secretary-General Joseph Wu and Chairman of the Ocean Commission Kuan Biling, and also visited Black Bear College and Cheng Nanrong Memorial Hall to have in-depth exchanges on topics such as democratic resilience, regional security, and the situation in the Taiwan Strait. (E)
0a356ea13742f850e5966a9a3a9bcc76d7eb4f3d: Wu Zhizhong, Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, hosted the "2026 China Influential Network Annual Conference" welcome dinner of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs
4745a47d7f08c71c1fdeddd30f4cbb49d5785099: 2026/07/08 Press Release No. 312 Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Wu Zhizhong hosted the "2026 China in the World Summit" welcome dinner of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs on July 6 today (2026). He interacted with the organizer of the annual conference, Taiwan Democracy Laboratory (Doublethink Lab), and representatives of think tanks, scholars, media and civil society organizations from around the world. The atmosphere was warm. Political Undersecretary Wu said in his speech that the current democratic society is facing challenges brought by the expansion of authoritarianism. In recent years, China has continued to expand its malicious influence through military threats, economic coercion, cyber attacks, disinformation, and transnational suppression. It recently enacted the National Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which has triggered concerns in the international community that it may institutionalize and normalize transnational suppression. Political Secretary Wu pointed out that the "China Influence Network Annual Conference" brings together experts and practitioners in various fields to help deepen understanding and exchanges on Chinese issues, and wished this year's annual conference a complete success. In his speech, Wu Ming-Hsuan, co-founder and CEO of Taiwan Democracy Laboratory, thanked the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for hosting the banquet and the enthusiastic participation of distinguished guests from all over the world. He also said that since its establishment, the "China Influence Network" has successively held annual conferences in Berlin, Germany, Taipei, Taiwan, and Johannesburg, South Africa. This year is the second time it is held in Taiwan. This year’s annual conference attracted more than 300 attendees, planned more than 60 breakout sessions and workshops, and was attended by about 100 domestic and foreign speakers. It is the largest conference in history. We hope that the distinguished guests can use this platform to fully discuss how to respond to the expansion of China’s influence. The "China Influence Network" is a global community launched by the Taiwan Democracy Laboratory in 2019. It aims to bring together civil society, academic institutions, researchers, media and policymakers from all over the world to study China's influence. It releases the "China Influence Index" (China Index) every year to evaluate China's influence and penetration in various countries from nine aspects including law, military, foreign policy, domestic politics, and economy. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to cooperate with similar-minded civil society groups such as the Taiwan Democracy Laboratory to jointly enhance democratic resilience and safeguard the rules-based international order. (E)
ef3bcaabc47eb39bb9f941fdd094c762c2d7586f: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs welcomes U.S. Senator Duckworth's visit to Taiwan
69f764c602f2c116d186ac1f9c45f222f5c48c53: 2026/07/06 Press Release No. 311 U.S. Senator Tammy Duckworth (D-IL) will visit Taiwan from July 6 to 8 this year (2026). During her visit to Taiwan, she will meet with President Lai Ching-te and high-level officials of the Chinese government. The two sides will exchange views on important issues such as Taiwan-US relations, regional security, economic, trade and technological cooperation, as well as the situation in the Taiwan Strait and Indo-Pacific region. Foreign Minister Lin Jialong expressed his sincere welcome to Senator Duckworth for his second visit. Senator Duckworth is a member of the "Senate Taiwan Connection" in the U.S. Congress. She has long supported deepening the Taiwan-U.S. partnership. In the past, she not only supported the friendship-Taiwan bill and signed a joint letter of friendship with China many times in the past, she also flew to Taiwan on a U.S. military plane when the COVID-19 epidemic in China was severe and the country was in urgent need of vaccines, announcing that the U.S. government would donate vaccines to Taiwan. This year coincides with the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States and the 30th anniversary of Taiwan's direct presidential election. Senator Duckworth and bipartisan senators jointly sponsored a resolution recognizing Taiwan's democratic achievements and reaffirming the United States' security commitment to Taiwan based on the Taiwan Relations Act and the "Six Assurances." He also co-sponsored the "STAND with Taiwan Act" (STAND with Taiwan Act) together with Senator Dan Sullivan (R-AK), hoping to use severe sanctions to deter China from invading Taiwan. Foreign Minister Lin Jialong thanked Senator Duckworth for visiting Taiwan again at this important moment, fully demonstrating his firm support for Taiwan. Illinois Treasurer Michael Frerichs and State Senator Paul Faraci will also participate in part of the group's itinerary, looking forward to jointly deepening connections between Taiwan and the United States at the local level. In the face of challenges such as the expansion of authoritarianism, gray zone coercion, and economic coercion, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to work closely with the U.S. Congress, executive departments, and friends from all walks of life on the basis of the existing good foundation to steadily promote the all-round partnership between Taiwan and the United States in the fields of security, economy, trade, and science and technology, and jointly create regional peace, stability, and prosperity. (E)
aff6ba7207623105795c47fb78f1343c00389b1e: Foreign Minister Lin Jialong held a luncheon to welcome the visit of a cross-party parliamentary group led by Speaker of the Congress of the Republic of Guatemala Contreras to Taiwan
e78c92c800ad5870f33e60b9758f28cf08c7ef7b: 2026/07/06 Press Release No. 310 Luis Alberto Contreras Colíndres, Speaker of the Congress of the Republic of Guatemala, led a cross-party parliamentarian group to visit Taiwan. Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Mingqi went to Taoyuan International Airport to greet him on the morning of July 6 this year (2026) on behalf of Minister Lin Jialong, expressing his sincere welcome from the Chinese government. Speaker Contreras arrived in Taiwan and delivered a speech, saying that this visit to Taiwan with members of parliament from all parties shows that supporting Taiwan-Guatemala friendship is the consensus of all parties in the government and the opposition, and that Guatemala will always be Taiwan's best friend. Members of the visiting delegation include Lucrecia Carola Samayoa Reyes, Second Secretary of the Presidency of the Guatemala Congress, Álvaro Enrique Arzú Escobar, Chairman of the Judicial Reform Committee of the Congress, Jorge Estuardo Ayala Marroquín, Chairman of the Economic and Foreign Trade Committee of the Congress, Jorge Mario Villagrán Álvarez, Chairman of the National Security Affairs Committee of the Congress, and Jairo Flores, General Convenor of the Opportunities and Unity Party of Congress. Joaquín Flores Divas), Congressman Héctor Adolfo Aldana Reyes, Chairman of the Congress Youth Committee, Congressman Jaime Octavio Augusto Lucero Vásquez, Congressman Manfredo Duvalier Castañón González and others. Minister Lin pointed out at the luncheon of Speaker Contreres' visit to the delegation on the same day that Guatemala has always been a solid friend who is sympathetic to Taiwan. The friendship between the two countries has entered its 92nd year. He thanked Speaker Contreres and the Guatemala Congress for sparing no effort in supporting our country's participation in international organizations and making a voice of justice for Taiwan. It was also emphasized that the Chinese government has adopted a "comprehensive foreign policy" policy and coordinated with Guarantee President Bernardo Arévalo's policy agenda to promote a number of "Prosperity Plans" in the fields of public health, infrastructure, higher education, and agricultural technology exchanges, with fruitful results. In addition, it will also expand incentives for Chinese businesses to invest in Guangdong, making Guangdong an important base for Taiwanese businessmen to expand overseas markets, strengthen economic and trade ties between the two countries, and realize the vision of mutual benefit and common prosperity. Speaker Contreras thanked Taiwan for its contribution in Guatemala, vigorously promoting many important projects such as public health, information technology, environmental disaster prevention and scholarships, assisting Guatemala's national construction and development and substantively benefiting the local people, fully demonstrating the spirit of sharing weal and woe between the two countries. Speaker Contreras also said that Taiwan has transformed from a developing country into a global center for technological innovation. This successful transformation experience deserves to be used as a model for international learning. As a loyal ally of Taiwan, Guatemala will continue to move forward hand in hand with Taiwan. During the banquet, the guests exchanged views with Luis Raúl Estévez López, Ambassador of Guatemala to Taiwan, Lin Ching-yi, Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Health and Welfare, Rongbang Economic and Trade Corporation, the Association for International Economic Cooperation of the Republic of China, National Taiwan University Hospital, Chunghwa Telecom, and my country's electric vehicles, to exchange opinions on Taiwan-Guatemala's core cooperation areas and economic, trade and investment issues. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to deepen exchanges, consolidate bilateral relations, and jointly seek benefits for the people of the two countries on the basis of the traditional friendly cooperation between Taiwan and Guarantee. (E)
3780ff8b5a7b2171d92b653ac9b043b8aafb54ab: In response to the incident in which senior media person Akio Yaita was deliberately followed and attacked by Chinese Hong Kong nationals in Taiwan, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs responded as follows:
a1cb574361b2b8f66c89ad59718affd20c450dd3: 2026/07/06 Senior media person Akio Yaita was attacked by Chinese Hong Kong citizens in Taichung on July 6 this year (2026), highlighting that China is carrying out transnational suppression against people of various countries around the world in an attempt to expand its authoritarian influence through violent acts. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs severely condemned this. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that the Chinese government's use of brutal means of "transnational suppression" to intimidate, harass, or interfere with the people of our country and other countries is a violation of international human rights, an infringement of the legal jurisdiction of various countries, and a serious violation of the bottom line of the rule of law. It should never be tolerated. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs noted that this case is the first case of transnational repression and violence after the implementation of China's National Unity and Progress Promotion Law, and called on the international community to jointly counter China's indiscriminate transnational repression and face up to the subsequent effects of China's evil laws and violence. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to coordinate closely with relevant government ministries and the international community to respond, strengthen the emergency response and rescue mechanism of foreign embassies, and strengthen cooperation with various countries to ensure the safety of foreigners traveling in and in Taiwan.
980902a5405393df3dbdf09ccadacef89ddd597f: Regarding China’s test-launch of strategic missiles into the Pacific on July 6, which disregards the importance of regional peace and stability, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs responded as follows:
84864b5b61b19b0084f4f793c67278f77694f8eb: 2026/07/06 China launched strategic missiles into the Pacific on July 6 this year (2026), undermining regional peace and stability and arousing concern from the international community. As the international community strives to maintain international peace and security, China has ignored regional stability and continued to carry out various actions that threaten the peaceful status quo. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses its solemn condemnation. In recent years, China has continued to use gray zone operations such as military intimidation, economic coercion and cognitive warfare in an attempt to unilaterally change the status quo in the Indo-Pacific. This not only escalates regional tensions, but also poses a serious challenge to the rules-based international order. China's strategic missile test this time once again highlights the use of military deterrence as a political tool, which not only does not contribute to regional peace and stability, but also deepens neighboring countries' concerns about China's expansion. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs emphasized that maintaining peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait and the Indo-Pacific region is a common interest of high global concern. Any act that unilaterally escalates tensions, undermines the status quo, or uses force to intimidate other countries is not in line with the expectations of the international community and the interests of all relevant parties, and runs counter to the purpose of the United Nations Charter to peacefully resolve disputes. China should exercise rational restraint and avoid taking any actions that may increase the risk of miscalculation or endanger regional security. As a responsible member of the international community, Taiwan will continue to work closely with countries with similar ideals to safeguard peace, stability and prosperity across the Taiwan Strait and the Indo-Pacific region.
82578f521f0d2c5e13b65c101151a4425dc9c459: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs sincerely welcomes the cross-party delegation of congressmen from the Republic of Panama
a958ae57a953124c70d68bd5a634796dc41a03e3: 2026/07/06 Press Release No. 309 Orlando Carrasquilla, Member of Parliament of the Republic of Panama and Chairman of the Committee on Agriculture and Fisheries Affairs, and a seven-member cross-party delegation of MPs visited Taiwan from July 6 to 10 this year (2026). The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed its sincere welcome and thanked the friends in the Panama Congress for their continued concrete actions to demonstrate their firm support for Taiwan. Panama is an important shipping and financial hub in the world and has active economic and trade exchanges with Taiwan. Last year, Taiwan was Panama's second-largest export market after the United States. The business opportunities between the two countries have great potential for sustainable development. The delegation is the third cross-party delegation organized by members of the Panamanian Congress in the past two years. It demonstrates the broad support of the Panama people for Taiwan and lays the foundation for cooperation and common prosperity between the two countries through practical and friendly exchanges in Congress. During their stay in Taiwan, the cross-party congressmen of Panama will meet with Vice President Hsiao Meiqin, receive a banquet from Foreign Minister Lin Jialong, visit Legislative Yuan Hanguo-yu, the Mainland Affairs Council, the Central Health Insurance Agency of the Ministry of Health and Welfare, the Water Conservancy Bureau of the Kaohsiung City Government, the International Cooperation and Development Foundation, and participate in Visits to Hsinchu Science Park, Kaohsiung Software Park, Kaohsiung Port, Beitou Incinerator and other facilities, through on-site visits to understand Taiwan's democratic and pluralistic society, the current political and economic development, and the role of high-tech industries in the global semiconductor supply chain, and continue to explore the possibility of cooperation between the two parties in various fields. (E)
e62306bc3f8400af28f23609c583d8a7f8c6e01e: Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Ming-chi attended the "Global Brainstorming Forum" and encouraged young students from National Taiwan University to create Taiwan's value
160bb3da9fdf4a4e446b0dbc2b31cc611672aea4: 2026/07/03 News Reference No. 054 Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Mingqi attended the closing ceremony of the 17th "Global Initiatives Symposium, GIS Taiwan 2026" organized by National Taiwan University on July 3 this year (2026), and delivered the speech "Bond and Beyond: Building Trust in a Divided World" (Bond and Beyond: Building Trust in a Divided World) World), he encouraged many young students at home and abroad to build a cross-border network of trust and cooperation in an international environment full of uncertainties, and jointly build a more resilient future for Taiwan. In his speech, Vice-Chancellor Chen echoed the theme of this forum "Bond and Beyond" and said that what is lacking in today's world is not connections, but "partnerships" that can withstand the test of crises, pressures and challenges; future international cooperation should not only pursue efficiency, but should also be based on trust, resilience and shared responsibility. Vice-Chang Chen pointed out that globalization has not ended but has entered a new stage of development. In recent years, including the epidemic, geopolitical competition, supply chain restructuring, energy security, and the rapid development of artificial intelligence, the international community has gradually shifted from pursuing efficiency and cost-effectiveness in the past to focusing on economic security, supply chain resilience, and trustworthy partnerships. How to build a resilient cooperation network has become an important issue faced by all countries. When talking about Taiwan's role, Chen said that the Ministry of Foreign Affairs has actively promoted the concept of "comprehensive diplomacy" proposed by Minister Lin Jialong in recent years, combining democratic values, partnerships with similar concepts, and Taiwan's technological and industrial advantages to deepen international cooperation, making Taiwan not only an important link in the global supply chain, but also a trustworthy, capable and willing international partner willing to share responsibilities. He emphasized that Taiwan continues to deepen cooperation with countries with similar ideals through the three major aspects of value diplomacy, alliance diplomacy, and economic diplomacy, working together to promote regional peace and stability, and enhance the resilience of democratic societies. Chen further pointed out that in the face of challenges such as authoritarian expansion, cognitive warfare, and disinformation, Taiwan has accumulated valuable experience in democratic governance and deeply understands that democratic resilience is not only a domestic issue, but also an important issue facing the world. Taiwan is willing to continue to combine its advantages in technology, talent, industry, and democratic systems to share experience and deepen cooperation with the international community to jointly respond to global challenges. Chen encouraged all young people participating in the forum to cherish the interpersonal connections established during the forum. Regardless of whether they invest in business, government, academic research, or civil society in the future, they should continue to cultivate the ability to cooperate and build trust across cultures, so that they can work together to overcome challenges and create greater value. The "Global Brainstorming Forum" was founded in 2008 and is independently organized by the student team of National Taiwan University. Over the years, it has invited young people from all over the world to gather in Taiwan to exchange and dialogue on important global issues. It is the largest international student academic forum in Taiwan, demonstrating the energy of Taiwanese youth to participate in global public affairs and promote public diplomacy. (E)
7c9e19031a23d1685cf25a6d66f66c2d039c1e49: Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade of the Republic of the Marshall Islands Mr. and Mrs. Kang Rende successfully concluded their visit to Taiwan, marking a new milestone in the Taiwan-Malaysia partnership.
368d4f2a46c6a329ef485563e7c6d533b796c9dc: 2026/07/03 Press Release No. 308 The delegation led by Hon. Kalani R. Kaneko, Minister of Foreign Affairs and Trade of the Republic of the Marshall Islands, a friendly country of our country, and his wife visited Taiwan successfully and departed Taiwan on the morning of July 3rd. Lin Zhaohong, Director-General of the Department of Asia, East and Pacific Affairs of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, saw off the plane on behalf of Minister Lin Jialong. During the visit, Minister Kang Ren-te and his wife not only met with President Lai Ching-te, but also attended the 30th anniversary celebration of the International Cooperation and Development Foundation, received a banquet from Foreign Ministry Minister Lin, and visited important figures from all walks of life such as the Ministry of Economic Affairs, the Financial Supervisory Commission and Air Asia, effectively promoting bilateral exchanges and cooperation and further deepening high-level interactions between Taiwan and Malaysia. In addition, the delegation also went to Shuanghe Hospital to inspect the results of the medical cooperation between the two sides, and went to the Marshall Islands Embassy in Taiwan to communicate and interact with international students in Taiwan. In addition, Minister Kang Rende and his wife personally experienced Taiwanese cooking classes, deeply experiencing Taiwan's diverse and humane food culture. The Republic of the Marshall Islands is an important friend of my country in the Pacific region, and both parties share core values ​​such as democracy, freedom and the rule of law. This visit not only further consolidates the profound friendship between the two countries, but also lays a more solid foundation for the two countries to deepen cooperation in various fields in the future. Taiwan and Malaysia will continue to work together to promote a mutually beneficial and co-prosperous partnership and work together to maintain peace, stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region. (E)
ca6f42df56a1c6fd09dac92b528498b4e226f1f4: The Chinese government will establish an "Office in Phoenix" in Arizona, USA, to further deepen exchanges and cooperation between Taiwan and the United States.
7edc74d47bd8f8241ea0f9a5de1043c11ef4224a: 2026/07/03 Press Release No. 307 The cooperation and exchanges between Taiwan and the United States in economy, trade, science and technology, education and supply chain continue to deepen. In order to strengthen services to Taiwanese businessmen and overseas Chinese in the United States and promote two-way investment between Taiwan and the United States, the Chinese government has decided to establish the "Taipei Economic and Cultural Office in Phoenix" in Phoenix, Arizona, United States. Preparatory work is currently in progress. Phoenix is ​​the capital of Arizona and an important and rapidly developing city in the southwestern United States. As China's semiconductor and related supply chain manufacturers continue to expand investment locally, Phoenix has gradually formed a large-scale and strategic technology industry cluster, becoming an important base for Taiwan and the United States to jointly build a trustworthy and highly resilient technology industry chain. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs decided to establish a new office in Phoenix after considering the new layout of Taiwan-US high-tech industrial cooperation, the increase in the number of Taiwanese businessmen and expats in Arizona and surrounding areas, and the increasingly close interaction between my country and US states and municipal governments. In the future, the office in Phoenix will help strengthen the relationship between China and the governments of Arizona and Phoenix, respond to the investment and operational needs of our businesses in a timely manner, and promote cooperation between the two parties in the fields of economy and trade, technology, supply chain, culture, education, and people-to-people exchanges. After the establishment of the Phoenix office, it will also provide more convenient consular services to overseas Chinese, Taiwanese businessmen and Chinese people in Arizona and New Mexico. In the future, the number of China's pavilions in the United States will reach 14, including the existing ones in Washington, New York, Boston, San Francisco, Los Angeles, Atlanta, Seattle, Houston, Chicago, Honolulu, Denver, Miami and Guam. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to work with the Representative Office in the United States and various offices in the United States to further deepen the mutually beneficial partnership between Taiwan and the United States on the existing good foundation, and promote local-level exchanges and industrial cooperation between Taiwan and the United States. (E)
6a59b443f640fd0e4a8511ffff858ef589e5c0b6: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs revised the subsidy measures for investment in friendly countries, expanded support for Taiwanese businessmen, and deepened the economic diplomacy of the "Rongbang Plan"
086228c76864bc846784a7f99c95b84699ec1c91: 2026/07/03 Press Release No. 306 In order to accelerate the promotion of the "Rongbang Plan", deepen economic and trade cooperation between my country and friendly countries, and encourage Chinese enterprises to invest in friendly countries, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs has revised the "Measures for Encouraging Enterprises to Invest in Countries with Diplomatic Relations" and will be promulgated and implemented on July 1 this year (2026). This revision of the law reduces the initial costs and investment risks for enterprises to enter emerging markets by relaxing subsidy projects, increasing subsidy amounts, and strengthening support for inspections in friendly countries. It encourages China's advantageous industries and the government to work together to develop in friendly countries, assist friendly countries to enhance industrial energy, expand international markets, further strengthen bilateral economic and trade cooperation, and implement the goal of mutual benefit and common prosperity. In order to implement the policy direction of "Comprehensive Diplomacy" and "Prosperity Plan", this amendment further strengthens the institutional support for enterprises to invest in friendly countries, hoping to attract more Taiwanese enterprises to invest in the friendly countries' markets, and combine China's advantageous industries such as smart agriculture, food processing, smart medical care, green energy, information communications, smart transportation and sustainable tourism with the development needs of the friendly countries to promote local investment and long-term operations, and transform Taiwan's industrial strength into an important force that deepens friendship and promotes the sustainable development of friendly countries. The key points of this revision are as follows: 1. Relax the scope of application of subsidy projects: Based on the actual investment needs of enterprises, the scope of application of subsidy projects will be expanded to include local employee salaries, factory equipment, business premises or land rent and financing interest, etc., and the original restriction of selecting one application will be removed to improve the flexibility and practicality of subsidy measures. 2. Increase the cumulative maximum subsidy amount: Considering that prices, labor costs and initial capital costs of enterprises’ overseas investments have continued to rise in recent years, the cumulative maximum subsidy amount for enterprises within 7 years will be increased from the original NT$20 million to NT$60 million to provide enterprises with more substantial and effective investment support. 3. Increase the subsidy for investment inspections in friendly countries: In response to the increase in international air tickets and transportation costs, the upper limit of air ticket subsidies for companies to go to diplomatic countries to inspect the investment environment, evaluate market conditions and negotiate cooperation opportunities will be increased to NT$100,000 to encourage businesses to actively evaluate their investment plans in friendly countries. In the face of global supply chain reorganization and industrial layout adjustment, AIA is not only an important diplomatic partner of Taiwan, but also has high potential for industrial cooperation, market expansion and regional layout. By amending the law, the policy support will be greatly enhanced, hoping to reduce the initial risks of enterprises' overseas investment layout, encourage more Taiwanese businessmen to invest and set up factories in friendly countries, establish supply chain nodes, and expand markets. It will also help friendly countries improve industrial technology, create local jobs and promote economic development, forming a win-win cooperation. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs emphasized that the core spirit of the "Rongbang Plan" is to gradually upgrade the traditional one-way assistance model to a partnership with investment, trade, industrial cooperation and common development as the core, and promote a new model of "allied countries and common prosperity". In the future, we will continue to combine the resources of China's diplomatic missions abroad, CCICED and relevant ministries and committees to assist Taiwanese businessmen to expand the markets of friendly countries, promote the industrial upgrading of friendly countries, expand exports and create jobs, so that Taiwanese enterprises can become important partners in deepening friendship and promoting common prosperity. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will continue to promote the "comprehensive diplomacy" policy, closely integrate my country's advantageous industries and civil society, deepen economic and trade cooperation with friendly countries through public-private collaboration, deepen bilateral friendship with substantial economic and trade momentum, assist Taiwanese businessmen to steadily deepen their global presence, and work with friendly countries to achieve a mutually beneficial and co-prosperous partnership. (E)

c9b249e0f2a7d2d08bc7b10a709d662316710455: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs solemnly opposes China's implementation of the so-called "National Unity and Progress Promotion Law" to carry out long-arm jurisdiction and transnational suppression, and calls on the international community to jointly counter the expansion of authoritarianism
12ae7057dedc51c0ea970ed5f15dc9e1d6671038: 2026/07/01 Press Release No. 303 In response to the Chinese government's implementation of the so-called "National Unity and Progress Promotion Law" on July 1 this year (2026), in an attempt to use its domestic laws and regulations to carry out long-arm jurisdiction and transnational suppression, expand threats and intimidation to the people of our country and other countries, and seriously infringe the sovereignty of various countries and international human rights, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses strong condemnation and solemn opposition to this. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs pointed out that the above-mentioned law, in the form of domestic law, clearly imposes jurisdiction over organizations and individuals outside China. It serves as the legal basis for China’s expansion of long-arm jurisdiction and transnational suppression, and poses a considerable threat to global freedom, democracy, and human rights. In the future, if people from various countries have relevant words and deeds that are not acceptable to China, they may be targeted or pursued under this law. The international community should jointly condemn China's once again authoritarian expansion of its domestic laws. In addition, many provisions of the law are based on China's so-called "undermining of ethnic unity," "creating ethnic divisions," and "unfavorable to ethnic unity and progress," and other concepts that lack clear definitions. They give China's law enforcement agencies room for arbitrary interpretation, making it difficult for the public to identify risks and thus conduct self-censorship, creating a chilling effect and seriously violating the principles of legal clarity and proportionality that should be valued in a country governed by the rule of law. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that Taiwan, the Republic of China, is a sovereign and independent country and is not affiliated with the People's Republic of China. None of China's domestic laws are binding on Taiwan, and it has no power to criminalize Taiwanese people. As a democratic and free country, Taiwan's people enjoy freedom of speech and thought in accordance with the law, and China does not tolerate interference or interference. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs emphasized that when authoritarianism exports fear, democratic countries must improve social resilience; when authoritarian forces try to create divisions, partners with similar ideals should deepen cooperation to jointly respond. As an important member of the democratic camp, Taiwan will continue to strengthen its social resilience through international cooperation and resist the Chinese government's transnational suppression. (E)
834165566b748c6d4decf040198405140521be24: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs' "Conditional Online Application for Passport Replacement" measure has been extended from 10 a.m. today to people whose passports are valid for less than one year. You are welcome to take advantage of it.

Qualified people are welcome to take advantage of it. For detailed instructions, please refer to the "Conditional Online Application for Passport Renewal Area" on the Global Information Network of the Bureau of Consular Affairs (Link: https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-418-7622-264e0-1.html). For event details and registration information, please refer to the "#FindingTAIWAN_2.0" event dedicated website (URL: https://findingtaiwan2026.com.tw/). The Ministry of Foreign Affairs sincerely invites domestic and foreign video creators and all friends who care about Taiwan to actively participate and share the stories of Taiwan and the world through creative audio and video content, so that the world can understand Taiwan from a more diverse perspective. (E)

Looking to the future, Taiwan will continue to work with the Holy See to deliver human-centered technological power to the corners of the world where it is needed most. Dean Chen Jianren of Academia Sinica and his wife also came to the celebration venue to express their congratulations. GWLS), and also visited Rome to meet with friends from the Italian Congress, think tank scholars and young students from Taiwan and Italy. 823cb31898f9911d3fc471c2ccfb344a4b9af505: At the invitation of the U.S. State Department, my country attended the "Silicon Summit" to deepen its supply chain partnership with "Silicon" partner countries in key strategic areas of AI.

f055fcfb1ca156f6e0709a40a66630bb31a49d01: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Ministry of Interior jointly organized the "2026 International Police Cooperation Forum" to join hands with other countries to strengthen their determination in police cooperation and improve the effectiveness of combating cross-border crimes.
02b4aac7bd4b9461f66610950c378d910a489506: 2026/06/25 Press Release No. 291 The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Ministry of Interior jointly held the "2026 International Forum on Police Cooperation—Combating Transnational Crime" from June 22 to 24 this year (2026). , and held an opening ceremony on the morning of June 23. Executive President Zhuo Rongtai, Minister of the Interior Liu Shifang, Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Wu Zhizhong, Director of the Police Department of the Ministry of Interior Zhang Rongxing, Palau Justice Minister Jennifer S. Olegeriil, International Association of Chiefs of Police (IACP) President David Rausch, Director of the Police Department of the Ministry of the Interior of Guatemala David Estuardo Custodio Boteo, and Chief of Police of the Eswatini Royal Police Manoma Vusie Masango and other 43 government ministers of justice and senior police officials were invited to attend. On behalf of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ge Baoxuan, Executive Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, welcomed senior police dignitaries from various countries, representatives of diplomatic envoys stationed in Taiwan, and police colleagues at a dinner on June 24. In his speech, Vice Minister Ge stressed that Taiwan has actively fulfilled its international obligations over the years and joined hands with other countries to combat transnational crime. He looked forward to further deepening cooperation networks with law enforcement agencies of various countries in key areas such as preventing fraud, money laundering, and drug smuggling. He expressed his sincere gratitude to the police and law enforcement agencies of various countries for establishing anti-crime partners with Taiwan. With strong support from various countries, Taiwan will continue to strive for meaningful participation in the International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL), expand cooperation with the international community, and jointly build a solid transnational security defense line. Palau Justice Minister Jennifer S. Olegeriil said in her speech that transnational threats require transnational solutions, and the international community must work together to combat organized crime, telecommunications fraud, drugs and other transnational crime issues. She also detailed the achievements of Taiwan-Peru cooperation, including the signing of the Taiwan-Peru Coast Patrol Cooperation Agreement in 2019, multiple joint exercises, law enforcement personnel exchange training, and Taiwan's donation of multi-functional patrol boats and the provision of drug detection tools, etc., which demonstrate that Taiwan is an important and trustworthy partner. David Rausch, President of the International Association of Chiefs of Police (IACP), emphasized that public security and transnational crime do not respect national boundaries. Only through transnational cooperation, the establishment of mutual trust relationships and real-time information sharing can law enforcement capabilities be effectively enhanced. This forum is conducive to experience sharing and practical exchanges between countries, and it is hoped that dialogue will be transformed into concrete and pragmatic cooperation results to continue to deepen global police cooperation and improve overall security. The "International Police Cooperation Forum" is an important police dialogue platform for my country to share and exchange crime prevention experience with other countries around the world and build a platform for combating cross-border crimes. It is also the largest annual gathering of high-level international police officials hosted by my country. This year, judicial and police chiefs and police officials from 43 countries are invited and crime prevention and control experts came to Taiwan for practical exchanges. In response to the latest transnational crime patterns and trends, they discussed forward-looking and pragmatic law enforcement strategies. Through deepening collaborative cooperation among police agencies of various countries, they strive to improve the effectiveness of crime prevention and prosecution, demonstrating the determination of China and other countries to jointly combat and prevent cross-border crimes. In the face of the rapidly evolving and increasingly complex complex crime patterns around the world, transnational law enforcement cooperation is crucial to maintaining public security. No country can face it alone. As a responsible member of the international community, Taiwan is willing and able to participate in the global law enforcement network and contribute to safeguarding social stability and the personal and property safety of all people. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs shoulders the responsibility of safeguarding the security of the people and safeguarding the values ​​of democracy and freedom. It works hard with various ministries and agencies to promote the signing of treaties and agreements with various countries on mutual legal assistance in criminal matters, police cooperation and combating crime. It also actively establishes cooperation mechanisms and information exchange platforms with various countries. Through close cooperation with international partners, it builds a more free, open, secure and stable international society, and implements the policy concept of comprehensive diplomacy. (E)

Fogel, Assistant Secretary of the Department of Commerce for Global Markets, and Daniel Whitley, Administrator of the Foreign Service Agency of the Department of Agriculture. The letter to business leaders affirms that Taiwan is a global leader in the digital economy and advanced manufacturing and has made important contributions to the prosperity of the United States and many economies; the letter to the governor encourages state and local governments to deepen cooperation with Taiwan on issues such as disaster preparedness, emergency response, and critical infrastructure resilience. This letter also pointed out that the Chinese embassy and consulates in the United States continue to contact U.S. state governments, local governments and enterprises in an attempt to obstruct their development of relations with Taiwan, and deliberately misinterpret U.S. policy towards Taiwan, falsely claiming that the United States has accepted Beijing’s specific position on Taiwan. Although Beijing continues to increase its pressure on China and put pressure on international companies, the U.S. government has clearly demonstrated its willingness to deepen cooperation with Taiwan, fully demonstrating the importance and solid foundation of the Taiwan-U.S. partnership. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs also expressed its high affirmation that the United States has clearly countered China's interference and pressure on normal exchanges between Taiwan and the United States, and reiterated that deepening cooperation with Taiwan is fully consistent with U.S. policy under the Taiwan Relations Act. Transnational repression has become an important issue of common concern for democratic partners such as the "G7" countries. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs also held a theme event on transnational repression through the Global Cooperation and Training Framework (GCTF) this week to work with partners with similar ideas to strengthen democratic resilience and counter authoritarian expansion and inappropriate intervention. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs emphasized that Taiwan is the Taiwan of the world and a trustworthy democratic partner. China continues to intrude and suppress, and attempts to restrict our country's economic and trade development and international space. Taiwan still relies on its sincere attitude towards friends, solid democratic resilience, and determination to maintain peace, stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region, and has won increasingly firm support from the international community. Taiwan has the ability and confidence to continue to move toward the world, and will continue to work hand in hand with the U.S. federal government, state governments, local governments, businesses, and civil society to promote a mutually beneficial "symbiotic partnership" and further deepen the solid friendship between Taiwan and the United States. (E)

3cbf332ad4a98a4c152bb54262a327067abf648c: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs thanks the United States for expressing concern over China’s intrusion into my country’s eastern waters
720eb9da91ed0d21b5d29be5712935b12ecd10c9: 2026/06/24 Press Release No. 288 The Ministry of Foreign Affairs noted that the Taipei Office of the American Institute in Taiwan (AIT/T) expressed concern about China’s dispatch of coast guard ships to my country’s eastern waters and harassment of merchant ships. The United States also clearly pointed out that China's assertion of jurisdiction over waters that Taiwan has peacefully managed for more than 70 years seriously undermines regional stability and is contrary to China's claim to pursue a peaceful resolution of disputes. The United States rejects any Chinese claims to interfere with freedom of navigation and overflight, the freedom to lay submarine cables, and other legitimate maritime rights, and urges China to stop using military, diplomatic, and economic means to pressure Taiwan and instead engage in meaningful dialogue with Taiwan's democratically elected government. Foreign Minister Lin Jialong expressed sincere welcome and gratitude to the United States for its firm support for Taiwan's security, peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, and international maritime order. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs pointed out that this (June) month, Chinese official ships have successively illegally intruded into my country's eastern waters and the waters around my country's Taiping Island, and improperly interfered with free navigation of merchant ships. Related actions that undermine regional peace, stability and international order have aroused serious concern from the international community. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that Taiwan, the Republic of China, and the People's Republic of China are not affiliated with each other and enjoy various rights and jurisdiction in my country's surrounding waters in accordance with relevant norms of international law and the law of the sea. China has no right to take illegal actions against any vessel sailing freely within its exclusive economic zone. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs calls on China to stop elevating threats to Taiwan and the region, as well as any actions that unilaterally change the status quo and undermine the international maritime order. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will also continue to cooperate with the United States and other countries with similar concepts to jointly safeguard legitimate maritime rights such as freedom of navigation and overflight, and safeguard regional peace, stability and prosperity. (E)
d2adcad27f3c2d42c1881040622a691d9e73633b: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs thanked the British Office in Taiwan, the French Institute in Taiwan and the German Institute in Taiwan for issuing a joint statement expressing concern about China's recent activities in the waters east of Taiwan.

Foreign Minister Lin Jialong expressed his sincere welcome and gratitude. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs pointed out that China has recently invaded Taiwan's eastern waters and harassed and interfered with passing ships. These "fake law enforcement, real power expansion" actions not only lack the basis of international law, but have also had a serious impact on regional security, freedom of navigation and international shipping order. Our country has repeatedly sternly condemned China’s attempts to use compound threats and other means to put pressure on Taiwan and further undermine regional peace and stability. The UK, France and Germany continued the spirit of this year’s G7 leaders’ statement on geopolitical issues and once again took concrete actions to demonstrate their support for maintaining peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait and the rules-based international order, fully reflecting that maintaining peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait and surrounding waters is the common interest of the international community. (E)
850201e7eeec2d7d005c9f79494cbc3d4956d2bc: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs severely condemns and refutes the false claim by China’s Taiwan Affairs Office that China’s reunification will not harm the legitimate interests of any country.
8304b6174047e56ee67c0b88c7e91230cc670522: 2026/06/24 Press Release No. 286 In response to the Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson's false statement on June 24 this year (2026) that "China's reunification will not harm the legitimate interests of any country, including its economic interests in Taiwan" and other statements without common sense in diplomacy, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs solemnly reiterated that the Republic of China and the People's Republic of China are sovereign and independent and not subordinate to the other. Taiwan is a democratic and free society. Taiwan's future is independently determined by the 23 million Taiwanese people, and China is not allowed to comment. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs emphasized that Taiwan interacts with other countries based on national sovereignty, participates in various diplomatic activities of the international community, and actively makes concrete contributions to the international community. It has become an indispensable and key partner in maintaining global peace and prosperity. It has never had to obtain China's consent or meet any preset political conditions from Beijing. Taiwan will continue to defend the national sovereignty and dignity of the Republic of China, expand and deepen substantive relations with other countries, and strive for more international participation to ensure peace, stability, and prosperity in the region and across the Taiwan Strait. (E)

Gabriel) and other friendly Taiwanese legislators have strengthened exchanges on Taiwan-Taiwan economic and trade, science and technology, culture and other issues. In addition to the aforementioned itinerary, Minister Lin also attended the signing ceremony of a cooperation plan between the Chinese Representative Office in Poland and the important Polish think tank "Casimir Pulaski Foundation" on the 22nd to enhance cooperation and exchanges between the two parties in the security field. 532adf84dfbb2819fcc6de44172f037221810eaf: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs releases the Taiwan Connects series of promotional videos to showcase the results of Taiwan's close connection and deepened partnership with Europe
3bcc38245672aa4948371fff1295aea12ed08720: 2026/06/24 Press Release No. 284 In order to demonstrate China’s concrete results in promoting Taiwan-EU relations in recent years, deepen European understanding of Taiwan, and enhance Taiwan’s international visibility, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs has developed the "Taiwan Connects" series of promotional videos on Taiwan-EU relations from the three major aspects of culture, technology, and value. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that the "Taiwan Connects" series of videos focuses on the increasingly deepening connections between Taiwan and Europe. Through warm and life-like narratives, it presents the interactions and results of the two sides in cultural exchanges, scientific and technological cooperation, and shared democratic values. It also conveys to the international community the diverse vitality of Taiwan's culture, the open spirit of democratic society, and Taiwan's active role as an important partner of Europe. The "Culture Chapter" uses elements such as beverage culture, music and traditional opera to present the cultural resonance of Taiwanese and European people across regions and languages. It also includes wonderful scenes of the Taiwanese performing arts team on the stage of the Paris Cultural Olympics, showing the deep connections established by cultural exchanges; the "Technology Chapter" focuses on Taiwan's technology industry and Europe The close interaction between Taiwan's society and industrial chain demonstrates the partnership between the two sides that continues to deepen cooperation in the fields of high-tech and future investment; the "Values" chapter shows that Taiwan and Europe share core values such as democracy, freedom, human rights, and the rule of law, and explains how these shared concepts have become an important basis for bilateral partnerships. The three videos in the "Taiwan Connects" series are all produced in full Chinese and English versions (approximately 4 minutes each), and edited into 1-minute and 30-second highlights. In addition, the 30-second version is available in four languages: French, German, Italian and Czech. In the future, it will reach European audiences through the in-flight entertainment system of national airlines and online distribution, and will be simultaneously released on the YouTube channel of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the "Taiwan Trend" YouTube channel, and official social platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Threads, and X. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and diplomatic missions around the world will also cooperate with appropriate activities and occasions to promote it, and welcome all walks of life to share and use it, hoping to let more international friends see the deep and diverse connections between Taiwan and Europe, and deepen the substantive partnership between Taiwan and Europe. (E)
f5ca44a321325e11e767b8318870bae862cfcc20: Foreign Minister Lin Jialong led an economic and trade delegation to attend the "2026 European Taiwan Image Exhibition" and "Taiwan Night" cocktail reception held in Poland, calling for jointly building a "Taiwan-Europe democratic supply chain" and promoting "2026 Taiwan Culture in Europe"
a67a06519efba451c2bfb15539439e64850ee2e2: 2026/06/23 Press Release No. 283 Foreign Minister Lin Jialong was invited to attend the opening event of the "2026 European Taiwan Image Exhibition" held in Warsaw, Poland on June 22 this year (2026), and participated in the "Taiwan Night" reception of the image exhibition in the evening of the same day. This image exhibition is the first time that Taiwan's important economic and trade flagship exhibition has been held in Warsaw. It has gathered more than 100 Taiwanese companies to participate in the exhibition and attracted more than 3,000 people from 47 countries to register to participate. It demonstrates Taiwan's strong strength in the field of forward-looking science and technology, as well as the continued deepening of Taiwan-EU economic and trade cooperation and supply chain partnerships. The opening ceremony of the event was attended by Polish and European dignitaries, including Michał Jaros, Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Economic Development and Science of Poland, and Valère Moutarlier, Deputy Director-General of the European Commission's Growth Agency, who attended and delivered speeches. Minister Lin delivered a speech at the opening ceremony of the "European Taiwan Image Exhibition 2026" and said that in the face of the dramatic changes in the geopolitical situation brought about by authoritarian expansion, the democratic camp has been deeply aware of the grim reality that excessive external dependence can easily be "weaponized" by opponents. This has also prompted Europe to fully accelerate the promotion of "re-industrialization" and "rearmament." At this critical moment when European countries are striving to strengthen "strategic autonomy" and build a "non-red supply chain," Taiwan, as a global technology hub, is absolutely an indispensable core strategic partner for Europe. Minister Lin called on Taiwan and Europe to jointly establish a "Taiwan-EU democratic supply chain" based on trust and shared values, and pointed out that Poland, as the core industrial and logistics hub of Europe, will play a pivotal role in this cooperation blueprint. Minister Lin further pointed out that as Taiwanese companies have deepened their presence in Europe in recent years, this cooperation vision is gradually being transformed into concrete reality. Taiwan-Poland bilateral economic and trade relations have recently reached milestones, including the Taiwan Electrical and Electronics Industry Association (TEEMA) announcing in March this year that it would establish a technology park in Poland. The core purpose of leading Taiwan's top industrial associations and technology companies to visit Poland is to accelerate the deepening of bilateral industrial connections and demonstrate Taiwan's solid technological strength to the international community. Finally, Minister Lin quoted the famous saying of former Polish President Lech Wałęsa that "without unity, there is no freedom" and called on the democratic camp to extend the "spirit of unity" to the economic and technological fields and work together to build a safe and resilient global supply chain. In his opening speech, Parliamentary Secretary Jarosz expressed his gratitude to Taiwan for choosing to hold the image exhibition in Poland. Poland is an important gateway for Taiwan to enter the European market. He hopes that this image exhibition will promote more Taiwan-Poland industrial cooperation and exchanges, emphasizing that Taiwan and Poland have a trustworthy and long-term partnership. In the face of today's turbulent international order, establishing a free, fair and democratic supply chain is a necessary condition. Poland will continue to deepen cooperation with Taiwan to jointly build a democratic supply chain. Cai Songhui, deputy secretary-general of the Electrical and Electronics Industry Association, announced in the opening speech that Miękinia, located in the Wrocław metropolitan area, will become a priority site for the "TEEMA Technology Park" and will focus on the development of artificial intelligence data centers, smart cities and electric vehicles. Minister Lin also positively affirmed the Association of Electrical and Electronics's specific plans to promote economic, trade and investment cooperation between Taiwan and Taiwan. On the evening of the same day, Minister Lin attended the "Taiwan Night" event, which invited more than 300 distinguished guests including Polish political and industrial representatives. Wonderful performances were performed by the Taiwanese performing arts group "Dancing Bell Theater" and the puppet theater group "Taiyang Theater", which showed that Taiwan not only possesses strong technological hard power, but also cultivates a profound and diverse social and cultural heritage. Minister Lin also introduced the drama "Taiwan Hometown Song" performed by the Taiwanese puppet theater group "Taiyang Troupe" on the spot. This is also one of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' "2026 Taiwan Culture in Europe" series of performances. Taiwan and Europe are not only important economic and trade partners, but can also deepen their cultural ties. (E)
606f3d5123bb5160bf3bef95a9a58c00d276e493: The Global Cooperation and Training Framework brings together global partners to strengthen democratic resilience and respond to the threat of transnational repression
5648388d1d781efb5891062ce82af72d381d694b: 2026/06/23 Press Release No. 282 In response to the increasingly severe threat of transnational repression, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, in cooperation with agencies stationed in Taiwan such as Canada, the United States, Japan, Australia, and the United Kingdom, and the Ministry of Justice, held the "Global Cooperation and Training Framework (GCTF) Building Resilient Democracy in Response to Transnational Repression" international event in Taipei from June 23 to 25, 2026. The workshop invited government officials, law enforcement officers, NGO representatives, and scholars and experts from 29 countries around the world to discuss how authoritarian regimes use digital technology, disinformation, cross-border coercion and other means to influence democratic societies, and to exchange experiences in legal systems, law enforcement cooperation, and victim protection among various countries. President Lai Chengde pointed out in his opening speech that transnational repression has become a major challenge faced by the world, and authoritarian regimes are undermining democratic systems through emerging technologies and information manipulation. President Lai said that Taiwan will continue to strengthen response measures from the three major aspects of "prevention, protection, and sanctions", strengthen support and protection for victims, and deepen cooperation with the European Union and other partners with similar concepts to jointly protect democracy, freedom, and universal values. Marie-Louise Hannan, representative of the Canadian Trade Office in Taipei, also affirmed Taiwan's democratic resilience and the achievements of citizen participation in her opening speech, and called on democratic partners to strengthen cooperation and exchanges to jointly respond to challenges posed by transnational repression and emerging technologies. This workshop focuses on the risks posed by the misuse of emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), deepfakes, and spyware, as well as the impact of disinformation and cognitive operations on democratic societies. Through case sharing and situational exercises, it will enhance countries' ability to prevent, identify, and respond to the threat of transnational repression, and further strengthen the resilience of democratic societies. Since its establishment in 2015, GCTF has held more than 100 international study camps and has become an important platform for exchanges and cooperation between countries with similar concepts. Taiwan will continue to deepen international cooperation through the GCTF, work with global partners to respond to transnational repression and emerging technological challenges, and jointly safeguard democracy, freedom and regional peace and stability. (E)
90090bcc70f21a6126a1e338f528905967154d9e: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs sincerely welcomes the delegation led by Solia, Speaker of Alto Paraná Province in Paraguay, to visit Taiwan.

Global), in order to learn from local successful practical experience and create an investment environment conducive to the entry of China's high-tech manufacturers. 8d11c0bf3cbf75e25489be4d32e23d96cbf4083a: Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Mingqi met with Paraguay’s Minister of Information, Communications and Technology Piard to discuss the AI ​​cooperation process between Taiwan and Paraguay
6b4f470f21996216b699ce7a577b1d1b6ef44842: 2026/06/20 Press Release No. 279: On June 18 this year (2026), Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Mingqi led a delegation to visit Paraguay, a friendly country in South America, and met with Gustavo Villate, Paraguay’s Minister of Information, Communications and Technology, to exchange views on the "Taiwan-Pakistan Sovereign AI Computing Power Center" process and related cooperation promoted by the two countries. Political Undersecretary Chen said that the "Memorandum of Understanding on Cooperation on the Taiwan-Pakistan Sovereign AI Computing Center Investment Project" was signed in May this year under the witness of the two heads of state, officially launching the plan to jointly build an AI computing center in Paraguay, and attracting the attention of international companies and financial institutions. Government Secretary Chen emphasized that Taiwan-Brazil AI cooperation is a key project of the "Rongbang Plan" promoted by Foreign Minister Lin Jialong. It will combine the high-level scientific and technological talents cultivated by the "Taiwan-Paraguay University of Science and Technology" and Huida founder Huang Jen-Hsun's "AI five-layer cake theory". The two parties will effectively convert Brazilian energy into computing power at the "energy, chip and infrastructure" level, thereby creating employment for Brazilian youth and injecting strong momentum into economic growth. Minister Biard agreed that the Taiwan-Pakistan AI cooperation initiative should be implemented as soon as possible. He emphasized that Brazil has abundant green electricity, which is sufficient to supply the computing power center operations. He hopes that the completion of this project will drive the transformation of Brazil's technology industry and become the focus of national development in the next generation. Political Undersecretary Chen also inspected the planned site for the first phase of the computing power center, inspecting the surrounding landforms, power distribution stations and flood control facilities. He also visited the Itaipú hydropower station, which supplies nearly 90% of Brazil’s electricity, to gain a practical understanding of Brazil’s power generation and supply situation, which serves as an important reference for promoting cooperation in AI computing power. The Taiwan-Pakistan AI Computing Power Center is an innovative cooperation that combines my country's high-tech strength with Pakistan's abundant green power. It is expected to target 10MW (millions of watts) of computing power in the initial trial phase, 100MW in the second phase, and 1GW (gigawatts) in the third phase. It will help deepen Taiwan-Pakistan's new generation technology and strategic industrial partnership. (E)

9fcb53611f0477ba3ca8796acde03b990b4df294: 2026/06/18 Press Release No. 276: On June 15 this year (2026), Parliamentary Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Chen Mingqi led a delegation to visit Paraguay, a friendly country in South America. 28d76ae530de0a3c4df30da2123fc58b12f32f71: Wu Zhizhong, Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, presided over the flag presentation ceremony of the "Soochow University WTO Moot Court Competition Team"
0f0a2bcb3fc17677a9b5e022eb120a01a43257a3: 2026/06/17 Press Release No. 274 On June 17, 2026, Parliamentary Secretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Wu Zhizhong hosted a flag-presenting ceremony for the Soochow University Law School team that will depart for Geneva, Switzerland, to participate in the "WTO Moot Court Competition World Championship" from June 23 to 27. He encouraged the players to shine for Taiwan on the international stage. Political Undersecretary Wu delivered a speech to congratulate the team for winning the Asia-Pacific regional competition, and wished them another good performance in the world competition. He also pointed out that the international situation is undergoing profound changes, from geopolitical competition, supply chain restructuring, to the rapid development of artificial intelligence. The global economic and trade order is facing new challenges and opportunities, and international rules and the rule of law are even more important. The world sees Taiwan not only because of Taiwan's strong technological and economic strength, but also because Taiwan is willing to become a trustworthy partner of the international community and adhere to a free, open and fair international economic and trade order. Wu Zhengci encouraged aspiring young people to become members of Team Taiwan of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, turning their expertise and ideals into the power to protect the country. In his speech, Legislator Wang Dingyu spoke from his own experience, saying that it is very hard for Taiwan to gain space for international participation. Colleagues from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs are fighting a smoke-free war every day. He encouraged students to be grateful to those who are working hard to defend the country. He hopes to learn from international competitions and grow stronger, become a force to protect the country in the future, and work together for Taiwan's diplomacy. Three experts including Ye Zhicheng, Director-General of the Department of International Cooperation and Economic Affairs of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Xie Peifen, Deputy Secretary-General of the International Cooperation and Development Foundation, and Wang Yuxiang, Assistant Executive Director of the International Economic and Trade Policy Research Center of the China Economic Research Institute, respectively responded to this year's competition topic "Eng eli─Critical Minerals Related Measures" and his own experience in participating in the competition put forward suggestions, encouraging students as outstanding economic and trade legal talents to not only understand the provisions, but also understand the national interests and international economic and trade realities behind the provisions, so that the discussion can have both legal depth and international pattern. The WTO Moot Court Debate Competition is hosted by the European Law Students Association (ELSA). It simulates the pleadings, defense and oral debates of the WTO dispute settlement mechanism in English. It is one of the most prestigious debate competitions in the field of international economic and trade law, and is divided into regional competitions and world competitions. In April this year, the Soochow University team stood out from 96 teams around the world and finished third in the Asia-Pacific region. Only 24 teams in the world qualified to advance to the world championship. Their outstanding performance deserves recognition. (E)
a9f20ab22c3c2faeca55640efd56bbff4e9bb7fd: Regarding the European Parliament's plenary session voting on the resolution "Countering Transnational Repression: Establishing an EU Strategy to Safeguard European Sovereignty and Democratic Values" and concerns about the Chinese government's pressure to terminate and cancel research and activities on Taiwan issues, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs responded as follows:
d5e9351f1e7e7f420d233f277033fa0d17616a86: 2026/06/17 The European Parliament plenary session voted on the resolution "Countering Transnational Repression: Establishing an EU Strategy to Safeguard European Sovereignty and Democratic Values" on June 16 this year (2026) to explore the nature and form of transnational repression and the EU's response policies, and to use various overseas organizations to implement Comprehensive and systematic surveillance, intimidation, and forced deportation, including abusing the INTERPOL mechanism, pressuring academics to cease research on supply chains, forced labor, or sensitive issues such as Tibet and Taiwan, and requiring artists to cancel activities involving Taiwan. The resolution calls on the EU and its member states to strengthen their coordination functions and deepen cooperation with partners with like-minded ideas on legislation, victim protection, accountability and sanctions. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs welcomed and affirmed this. Transnational repression has become an important challenge for authoritarian regimes to democratic countries and civil society. It not only infringes on freedom of speech and academics and the protection of human rights, but also threatens democratic systems and social resilience. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs welcomes the European Parliament's attention to relevant issues and supports the EU in strengthening its response mechanism. In the future, it will continue to deepen cooperation and exchanges with the EU and other countries with similar ideas in the fields of information sharing, building democratic resilience, and countering authoritarian coercion.

7d43fd5de1c2adbd4eaf3cfd8b354bf2f2a11e86: Relevant domestic media quoted a report from the South African-based media "Swaziland News" on June 13, alleging that China's ambassador to the Kingdom of Eswatini, Liang Hongsheng, was suspected of lobbying Swaziland MPs to remove Swaziland Prime Minister Russell Dlamini through a vote of no confidence. It also claimed that Taiwan used aid such as rice to help MPs win elections and then defend Taiwan-Historic relations in Congress. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs solemnly responded as follows:
