c7b0bdc121538b881bf229ef094dd6d76125fab2: The Taiwanese government of the Republic of China expresses its deep condolences on the death of Princess Pracharajitiyapa, the eldest daughter of the King of Thailand
7fc1fe7d5d850fff700103bb0cf11f9880654bf7: 2026/06/16 Press Release No. 271 The Taiwan Government of the Republic of China expresses its deep condolences and sincere condolences on the death of Her Royal Highness Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendiradebyavati Kromluangrajasarinisiribajra Mahavajrarajadhita, the eldest daughter of His Majesty King Maha Vajiralongkorn. President Lai Ching-tak and Chief Executive Officer Toh Eng-thai have respectively conveyed the condolences of the Chinese government and people on the passing of Princess Pracharakitya through the Chinese Representative Office in Thailand. Ge Baoxuan, Executive Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, also went to the Thailand Trade and Economic Office (Thailand Trade and Economic Office) on behalf of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in the morning of June 16 this year (2026) to pay tribute and deliver a condolence speech. Princess Pacharakitya has dedicated her life to the Thai country and society. She has served as a prosecutor in the Supreme Prosecutor's Office of Thailand and the Thai Ambassador to Austria and other important public offices. She has long promoted the advancement of women and disadvantaged groups. Her outstanding wisdom and compassion are deeply admired and loved by the Thai people. The Thai cabinet has ordered a 15-day memorial period across the country. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs calls on people traveling in Thailand to follow the local royal family’s funeral customs to show respect for Thailand’s atmosphere of mourning. (E)
1014c50c2ac90c10c9a4174aa283d942224941b3: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs strongly protested and severely condemned China's pressure on Kenya to barbarously obstruct Chinese scholars from attending this year's "11th Our Ocean Conference" (OOC 11)
989df1e3e2bab1b1401a169bdbe0a611d9aa5e2a: 2026/06/16 Press Release No. 270 Chinese scholars were invited by the Kenyan government, the host country of the 11th Our Ocean Conference (OOC 11) this year (2026), to attend the pre-conference academic exchange meeting in Mombasa (Mombasa) on June 14 this year and publish professional research reports. When I went to the venue to register for admission, the organizer refused to issue an admission pass on the grounds that it did not recognize the Chinese passport. The Kenya Immigration Bureau intervened forcefully. Despite our efforts to negotiate, the passport and mobile phone were still detained and kept for more than 20 hours before they were allowed to fly out of the country. The China Maritime Commission originally organized a delegation to participate in the meeting as usual. After considering the extremely unfriendly measures of the Kenyan government and the personal safety of our delegation members, we decided to cancel the meeting. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed strong protest against China's pressure on the Kenyan government to refuse Chinese scholars from attending international maritime academic exchange conferences, and to detain passports, mobile phones, restrict personal and communication freedoms and other barbaric actions that violate human rights and international practices, and severely condemned them. As an indispensable and important global partner in promoting ocean sustainability, my country has participated in the "Our Ocean Conference" (OOC) since 2015. In addition to actively promoting exchanges and cooperation in international ocean affairs by adhering to the principles of "professionalism, pragmatism, and contribution", it also encourages the academic community to participate in sharing my country's efforts and research results in marine ecological conservation. It is reported that the Kenyan government hosted the OOC this time, but faced strong interference and pressure from China. It ignored our country’s conventions and outstanding contributions over the years, excluded us from participating, and even detained Chinese scholars who participated in the meeting. This once again exposed China’s arrogant and domineering diplomatic behavior and political thinking that politics overrides the professionalism of ocean sustainability. As the host country of OOC, Kenya has willingly become China's political thug and blatantly violates the principles of tolerance and cooperation promulgated by OOC. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed strong protest against this. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs severely condemned China's actions of suppressing China's international participation to the point of a gangster siege, and sternly demanded that China stop its barbaric behavior that undermines cooperation in global maritime affairs. It also called on allied countries to take it seriously and jointly curb China's intensified bullying diplomacy. As a maritime country, Taiwan has always been actively involved in global marine ecological conservation and sustainable environmental development. It will neither fear nor succumb to China's arbitrary and domineering behavior. It will continue to work together with the international community and strive to promote cooperation on global maritime affairs. (E)
465415830b6e3cd3aaa9e8bbbad8a8e72d90aa19: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs solemnly refutes the untrue fallacy released by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China that the content of the talks between Nepali Foreign Minister and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi involved derogating my country’s sovereignty.
bfa24a9f4dd16dbe8a84c9c58e14c986580f3849: 2026/06/16 Press Release No. 269 The Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs released news on the talks between Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Nepalese Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal on June 15 this year (2026). The Ministry of Foreign Affairs once again sternly refuted and condemned the fact that Wang Yi still ignored international objective facts and falsely claimed the fallacy of the "one-China principle" during his interaction with the Nigerian Foreign Minister K. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that Taiwan, the Republic of China, is a sovereign and independent country and is not affiliated with the People's Republic of China. Any untrue statement that attempts to disparage Taiwan's sovereign status cannot change this indisputable fact. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs also warned the new government of Nepal that it should seriously face China's long-term threat to peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, which is an indispensable key to the security and prosperity of Nigeria and the world. It should also recognize as early as possible the realities of China's expansionism in many regions and countries around the world, forcing many countries into debt traps through various improper means that lack good governance and democracy, and China's persecution of ethnic minorities and suppression of religious freedom. Therefore, adhering to the "One-China Principle" that China uses to undermine peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait will only harm but not help Nigeria. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that Taiwan is willing to continue to cooperate with all countries that support freedom, democracy, and human rights to jointly and firmly safeguard peace and security across the Taiwan Strait, and to jointly safeguard freedom and stability in the Indo-Pacific region. (E)
df4da189b60794425df72637607ae52571318605: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs welcomes the delegation led by McCobbe, Vice Chairman of the Congressional African-American Congressional Connection of the U.S. House of Representatives, to visit Taiwan
1c2a00c1f6f85cc04337345b13a85d3da1306c9f: 2026/06/16 Press Release No. 268 Lucy McBath (D-GA), Vice Chairman of the Congressional Black Caucus (CBC) of the U.S. House of Representatives, and Representative Valerie Foushee (D-NC), a CBC member, visited Taiwan from June 16 to 20 this year (2026). During their stay in Taiwan, the delegation will meet President Lai Ching-te and Vice President Hsiao Meiqin, and will be hosted by Foreign Minister Lin Jialong for a banquet. The two sides will exchange views on important issues such as Taiwan-US security, economy and trade, and regional situations. Foreign Minister Lin Jialong expressed his sincere welcome to Vice Chairman Ma Kebei and his delegation. The "African Congressman Connection" was established in 1971 and has long been concerned about the civil rights of African Americans, economic development, social welfare, judicial reform and other issues. In addition, Vice Chairman McCabe and Representative Fu Xi also paid attention to important issues such as artificial intelligence (AI), semiconductors, high-tech talent cultivation and human rights, which are important issues for continued exchanges and deepening cooperation between Taiwan and the United States. This trip is the first visit to Taiwan by the two congressmen during their term of office. This year not only coincides with the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States and the 30th anniversary of the direct election of Taiwan’s president, but June 19 also marks Juneteenth, which highlights the United States’ pursuit of equality and human rights. The delegation’s visit at this time also demonstrates that freedom and human rights are important shared values ​​​​between Taiwan and the United States. Minister Lin Jialong welcomed friends in the U.S. Congress to continue to deepen exchanges with Taiwan. Taiwan and the United States are important security and economic and trade partners of mutual benefit, and the two sides continue to deepen cooperation in areas such as security, technology, and supply chain resilience. Taiwan will steadily advance Taiwan-U.S. partnerships in various fields based on the existing good foundation, and focus on strengthening self-defense capabilities and the resilience of society as a whole, safeguarding hard-won democracy, and continuing to work hand in hand with the United States and other countries with similar ideals to jointly promote the well-being of the people and maintain peace, stability, and prosperity across the Taiwan Strait and the Indo-Pacific region. (E)
31a70848059bf503935119fbbf429e37deae4294: Taiwan, the United States, and Palau once again co-organized the "Global Cooperation and Training Framework" overseas activities to strengthen Palau's economic security and investment resilience
fc4171778a207ec2f23172ab2ef95901821b6018: 2026/06/15 Press Release No. 267 The embassies of my country and the United States in Palau cooperated with the Ministry of State and Justice of the Republic of Palau to jointly host the "Global Cooperation and Training Framework" (GCTF) international training camp in Palau on June 11, 2026. With the theme of "Enhancing Economic Investment in Palau", this event invites government officials, industry, government, academia, experts and scholars from countries with similar ideas to participate, to exchange experiences from various countries on topics such as promoting sustainable foreign investment and strengthening economic resilience, and to explore how to balance transparent governance and the security of key industries while promoting economic development. Chen Gangyi, Ambassador of my country to Palau, said in his opening speech that through the "Rongbang Project", Taiwan is assisting Palau in developing power infrastructure, green energy and ecological tourism, promoting sustainable smart islands, and deepening judicial cooperation to jointly safeguard economic and social security. Landisang Kotaro, Chief of Staff of the Office of the President of Palau, also emphasized that transparent governance and trustworthy partnerships are the key to attracting high-quality investment, promoting sustainable development, and maintaining stability in the Indo-Pacific region. During the study period, Palau Justice Minister Jennifer Olegeriil, Director of the Bureau of Foreign Investment Raymond August, and Foundation for Defense of Democracy senior researcher Cleo Paskal were arranged to deliver special speeches on economic investment and national security, foreign investment regulations, and geopolitical issues respectively. In addition, the event also discussed how to strengthen institutional capabilities, improve investment risk assessment mechanisms, and promote cooperation between the public and private sectors and international partners through discussions and roundtable forums. Since its establishment in 2015, the Global Cooperation and Training Framework (GCTF) has become an important platform for Taiwan, the United States and countries with similar philosophies to share professional knowledge, strengthen capacity building, and respond to common challenges. This event held in Palau demonstrates the joint commitment of all parties to promote transparent investment and sustainable development, and demonstrates Taiwan's efforts to work with democratic partners to maintain security, prosperity, and a free and open order in the Pacific region. (E)

72706d7294bce9a86fd529376ea07cb967327499: Executive Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Ge Baoxuan attended the "128th Anniversary of Philippine Independence" National Day reception
f7a33ef0a4b74e40bc125043c42df743abaf56ca: 2026/06/13 Press Release No. 265 Executive Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Ge Baoxuan attended the "128th Anniversary of Philippine Independence" National Day reception hosted by the "Manila Economic and Cultural Office (MECO)" on the evening of June 12 this year (2026). Deputy Minister Ge first expressed sincere congratulations to the people and government of the Philippines on behalf of Foreign Minister Lin Jialong, and pointed out that Taiwan and the Philippines share the cultural roots of the Austronesian language and have close and friendly relations. After the two sides granted each other visa-free treatment, the Philippines has stably become the largest source of tourists from Southeast Asia to Taiwan, and has also become the ninth destination country for Chinese people to travel overseas. He looks forward to the continued implementation of the mutual visa-free program between Taiwan and the Philippines. Vice Minister Ge also pointed out that Taiwan is the top 10 trading partners of the Philippines, and the two countries have close substantial exchanges in various fields. There are currently more than 170,000 Filipino citizens working hard in Taiwan and contributing to the development of Taiwan's industry. Under the policy framework of "comprehensive diplomacy," China will actively participate in the key industry supply chain cooperation between the Philippines and the United States in the "Silicon Initiative" to gradually implement various plans for the "Taiwan-Philippine Economic Corridor." Deputy Secretary Ge further emphasized that my country and the Philippines share common interests and strategic vision. As important members of the first island chain, both parties are not only committed to maintaining freedom, democracy, and a rules-based international order, but also recognize the importance of maintaining regional peace and stability. They also support the peaceful resolution of disputes in accordance with international law. We firmly believe that under this shared concept, Taiwan will continue to expand and deepen cooperation with the Philippines in the fields of economy and trade, culture, education, maritime affairs, tourism, humanitarian assistance, disaster prevention and relief, and jointly create a prosperous future for the two peoples. In his speech, Corazon A. Padiernos, Chairman of the Manila Economic and Cultural Office and Representative in Taiwan, first expressed his deep condolences to the victims of the recent earthquake in Mindanao, Philippines. He then stated that Taiwan and the Philippines are important partners in the fields of economic and international cooperation, and thanked Taiwan society for its acceptance and support of the 170,000 Filipino workers in Taiwan. Chairman Peng also took this occasion to express special gratitude to Taiwan for its selfless and immediate medical and humanitarian assistance to the Philippine people. When many countries refused to provide medical diagnosis and treatment for difficult cases, only Taiwan was willing to lend a helping hand. This is a concrete demonstration of the continued deepening of bilateral relations and closer exchanges. Chairman Peng finally wished in Chinese that the friendship between Taiwan and the Philippines would last forever. This year, the Manila Economic and Cultural Office used the theme of "Weaving Craft" (tennun) to highlight the cultural similarities between Taiwan and the Philippines. It also carefully displayed the Philippine cultural heritage "tapestry" and traditional dance performances on site to showcase the historical and cultural origins and connections between Taiwan and the southern island of Philippines. (E)
a2cc9d1d44a1b0d6f12e9f09468d854bb6160d63: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs strongly condemns the first blatant intrusion of Chinese official ships into the waters of Taiping Island, and calls on the international community to jointly counter China's illegal acts

A series of harassment behaviors pose a serious threat to my country’s sovereign rights and interests, maritime security, and regional peace. The Chinese government's unreasonable actions have seriously violated international law and the Convention on the Law of the Sea, and are a blatant provocation to the international order. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs severely condemned China's illegal actions that violated our country's sovereignty, undermined regional peace and stability, and challenged the international order. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterated that Taiwan, the Republic of China, and the People's Republic of China are not affiliated with each other. The South China Sea Islands belong to the territory of the Republic of China. The Republic of China enjoys rights under international law and the law of the sea over the South China Sea Islands and related waters, which cannot be questioned, let alone challenged. The intrusion of Chinese official ships into the waters of Taiping Island not only seriously infringes upon my country's sovereignty, but also endangers the personal safety of our ships and personnel. China's malicious provocations and barbaric acts seriously violate the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea and international laws and regulations related to maritime security, blatantly challenge the international order, and undermine regional peace, security and stability. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs once again calls on the international community to face up to the serious and immediate harm caused by China's illegal actions to regional peace and stability, and to jointly counter China's illegal actions. my country is a party that maintains peace and stability in the South China Sea, and the government will continue to work hard to protect maritime security and continue to seek support from countries with similar ideals to jointly safeguard Indo-Pacific security with my country. (E)

1d21d694f2526a6ba673f840165ff21cf53dc1ae: Chen Ming-chi Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs Republic of China (Taiwan) September 22, 2025 (As Prepared for Delivery) President Tseng Yung-chuan of the ROC Chapter of the WLFD; The Honorable Dennis Rogan, Deputy Speaker of the UK House of Lords; The Honorable Diarmuid Wilson, member of the Irish Senate; The Honorable Tina Rose Muña Barnes, Senator of the Guam Legislature; The Honorable Bruce Knotts, President and CEO of the NGO Committee on Disarmament, Peace, and Security; Excellencies Ambassadors and Honorable Representatives; Distinguished participants; Ladies and gentlemen: Good morning! On behalf of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, I would like to extend a warm welcome to our dear friends from around the world attending today’s event. It is a great privilege to be invited to speak here today. I am delighted to witness the outstanding achievements of the WLFD and its strong dedication to upholding our shared beliefs. The world is facing more daunting challenges than ever. Authoritarianism and collusion among autocratic regimes have been spreading, thereby eroding our values and the rules-based international order. This development has further divided free and open societies and disrupted global peace and prosperity. These challenges may be political, military, economic, social, or even hybrid in nature. They threaten to undermine our defense mechanisms. External forces frequently enlist the aid of local collaborators who engage in influence operations and disinformation campaigns so as to generate turmoil and distrust within our societies. All of us must remain highly vigilant in response to such efforts. Taiwan has long stood on the front line defending freedom and democracy from authoritarian expansionism. Over the years, it has had to defend against attacks on its sovereignty and economy and respond to the denial of its fundamental right to participate in international initiatives. Nevertheless, Taiwan has continued to serve as a beacon of freedom, democracy, and resilience, earning respect worldwide. Taiwan is a responsible member of the international community and plays a pivotal role in global supply chains. It remains committed to working with like-minded partners such as the WLFD in safeguarding shared values and protecting common interests. Indeed, Taiwan has been and will continue to be a force for good, advancing pragmatic cooperation and value-added diplomacy to ensure a prosperous and sustainable future for everyone. Taiwan needs the world, and Taiwan matters to the world. This is not a political statement but rather a reflection of reality. In recent years, Taiwan has become an important hub for international nongovernmental organizations (INGOs) that promote democracy and human rights in Asia. Many INGOs have opened offices in Taiwan to further their reach. These include Freedom House, the Westminster Foundation for Democracy, the National Democratic Institute, the International Republican Institute, and the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom. We look forward to welcoming even more INGOs setting up operations here in the future, as they truly are the catalyst for promoting world peace. In closing, your presence underscores our democratic unity. We must continue to work in solidarity to make the world a better place for future generations. Taiwan is proud to lead the league of freedom and democracy, uphold the principles of liberty and inclusiveness, and promote prosperity and peace for all. I wish you all a pleasant and productive stay, and this conference every success. Thank you.
1daa66d9530839f02f204edb24acc785dc9dbe47: François Chihchung Wu Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs Republic of China (Taiwan) August 5, 2025 (As Prepared for Delivery) Distinguished guests; Ladies and gentlemen: Bonjour, good afternoon! It is a great privilege to introduce our keynote speaker for this afternoon’s session, the Honorable François de Rugy. He is an exceptional leader, a dedicated advocate for Taiwan–France relations, and a personal friend, un ami auquel je tiens beaucoup! During my six years serving as Taiwan’s Ambassador to France, I had the honor of witnessing first-hand François’s outstanding leadership and his unwavering support for democratic values. As Minister d’Etat, Minister of State, number 2 in the French government, he received then–Minister Gong Mingxin of Taiwan’s National Development Council — the first time a French Minister of State welcomed a Taiwanese ministerial-level official in his ministry — a gesture that spoke volumes about his friendship and support for democratic Taiwan. François has also served as President of the National Assembly and multiple terms as a member of parliament, these experiences have made him a strong and respected voice for reform and progress, not only in France, but also around the world -- and very much here in Taiwan! As a friend of Taiwan, François has played a vital role in strengthening the partnership between our two nations. He first visited Taiwan in 2016 during his tenure as Vice President of the National Assembly, marking the beginning of a deep and lasting engagement. In 2020, he became Chairman of the National Assembly’s France–Taiwan Parliamentary Friendship Group, working tirelessly to foster closer relations. Thanks to his leadership, in 2021 the French National Assembly overwhelmingly passed its first-ever resolution supporting Taiwan’s participation in international organizations. Later that year, François returned to Taiwan to further solidify our ties. Today, François will draw on years of experience to analyze the current geopolitical landscape and explore strategies to address global challenges. I am confident that His insights will help us navigate the complexities ahead and advance our shared vision. And now, please join me in giving a warm welcome to the Honorable François de Rugy — a true friend of Team Taiwan and Team Freedom. François, sachez que ton amitié compte beaucoup pour nous. Merci!
e3a5c0866d9ee86610835998f0bffec553ad4358: The minister received an English speech by Pholile Shakantu, Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of the Kingdom of Eswatini.
ad6e24e30b2a253b9ef9bbc77e3f2ae658597b52: Dr. Lin Chia-lung Minister of Foreign Affairs Republic of China (Taiwan) April 24, 2025 (As Prepared for Delivery) Ladies and gentlemen: Good morning! The opening of the 5G Government Network and Smart City Development Project operations center is a milestone in Eswatini’s national development. It is an honor to be jointly hosting this ceremony with Prime Minister Dlamini (Lamini). We are witnessing an important step toward achieving His Majesty King Mswati (m-swa-ti)’s vision of making Eswatini a first-world nation. ICT is developing at a rapid pace. Digital and smart technologies are improving the quality of life, driving social progress, and creating economic growth. They have become a part of our everyday lives. Taiwan is known as a smart technology island. We have worked hard to develop reliable networks, reinforce digital governance, safeguard digital sovereignty, and build smart cities. In these areas, we have achieved some success and improved the well-being of our people. As an active and responsible member of the international community, Taiwan is keen to share its achievements with allied nations. During his tenure as CEO of the National Disaster Management Agency in 2022, Prime Minister Dlamini took part in Taiwan’s Advanced Training Program on Disaster Prevention Technology and Management. Last month, Mayor Mkhonta (m-kon-ta) and CEO Fakudze (fa-ku-ze) attended the Smart City Expo in Taiwan, where they had the opportunity to see developments and applications of smart city initiatives. Taiwan and Eswatini are working closely together on the 5G Government Network and Smart City Development Project. This initiative will support economic and social development in Eswatini. It will create a dedicated 5G network for the government to ensure digital sovereignty and maintain network security. Transportation, public safety, and disaster prevention systems will be connected with 5G infrastructure. This will transform Mbabane into a smart city. The program will also increase the efficiency of urban governance. The capital city of Mbabane is the starting point. Moving forward, the project will be expanded to other major cities. Eswatini is on a path to becoming a 5G nation with first-world status. This project shows that Taiwan can help. It also demonstrates that Taiwan-plus-Eswatini cooperation is greater than the sum of its parts. Guided by the spirit of Nkwe, Taiwan will continue to collaborate with Eswatini. We plan to introduce digital and smart technologies into other sectors such as health care and business. Our goal is to create a technological Taiwan and a smart Eswatini. Let us keep working together to improve the well-being of our people. Thank you.
4471ed53913548ab900c984cf499b8fa1bc467d8: Dr. Lin Chia-lung Minister of Foreign Affairs Republic of China (Taiwan) February 24, 2025 (As delivered) Ladies and gentlemen: Good afternoon! It is a great pleasure to attend the 2025 MOFA Taiwan Fellowship Lunar New Year reception. On behalf of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA), I want to extend my warmest welcome to all of the distinguished Taiwan Fellowship scholars. I would also like to express my sincere appreciation to Director General Wang and her capable staff for organizing today’s event. Without your hard work, today’s event would not have been possible. MOFA established the Taiwan Fellowship in 2010. This program aims to attract exceptional scholars and experts from around the world to come to Taiwan and conduct research related to our country, cross-strait relations, and the Indo-Pacific region. To date, over 1,500 scholars have benefited from this program. Since assuming the post of foreign minister, I have increased the number of recipients from 100 to 125, demonstrating MOFA’s strong commitment to the program. This year, we are pleased to welcome scholars from 41 countries. Academics conducting research here not only serve as important ambassadors between Taiwan and their home countries but also link our country to the rest of the world. You all play a vital role in influencing how people see Taiwan by sharing your experiences here with people back home or on various platforms such as articles or social media. We encourage all of you to share stories of your time in Taiwan, speak up for our country, and help the world gain a better understanding of Taiwan. MOFA is currently implementing integrated diplomacy policy and the Diplomatic Allies Prosperity Project. Academic communities and people-to-people networks are important pillars of our efforts. Knowledge sharing is critical for a country’s national development. I therefore look forward to exchanging ideas with all of you. This year, we celebrate the Year of the Snake. The snake is a creature that represents intelligence, flexibility, and dynamic energy. My wife and I wrote a spring couplet, which translates as “deftly reaching far, smoothly realizing This conveys our expectations for the new year. By being as flexible and wise as a serpent, we can surely overcome all challenges. Today’s reception provides you the chance to connect with other fellowship recipients, domestic scholars, the ambassadors or representatives of your home countries, and the administrative staff of everyone in this program. of the Snake. Happy New Year! Thank you.
30fcd4002b309b4106592a96a50ceb5c6d50c0a5: Dr. Lin Chia-lung Minister of Foreign Affairs Republic of China (Taiwan) January 17, 2025 (As Prepared for Delivery) Ladies and gentlemen: It's my great honor to lead a delegation to the Republic of Palau on behalf of President Lai to attend the inauguration ceremony of President Surangel S. Whipps, Jr. and Vice President Raynold “Arnold” Oilouch. I met dignitaries from around the world, such as H.E. Hilda Heine, President of the Marshall Islands, H.E. Feleti Penitala Teo, Prime Minister of Tuvalu, H.E. Wesley W. Simina, President of the Federated States of Micronesia. The heads of state and ministers from the Pacific region gathering here show that all countries attach great significance to the inauguration ceremony. Since I took office as Minister of Foreign Affairs, I have proposed "Integrated Diplomacy" which constitutes three pillars: values-based diplomacy, alliance diplomacy and economic and trade diplomacy. We aim to utilize Taiwan’s model to help our brother Palau, develop its economy by promoting green tourism, clean energy, smart islands and other smart industries. I take advantage of this opportunity to invite business representatives from different industries in Taiwan to visit Palau with me to explore investment opportunities. Over the past two days, business leaders visited many places like Babeldaob, Ngatpang harbor, Malakal Port and Rock Islands Southern Lagoon. The excursions not only demonstrate how beautiful Palau is, but also afford us a chance to observe where business opportunities lie. Palau is full of possibilities when it comes to developing green, clean, and sustainable business. I believe that through our “Diplomatic Allies Prosperity Project”, Taiwan and Palau can forge a win-win future based on our shared values of freedom and democracy. Our two nations established diplomatic relations in 1999. Over the past quarter century, Palau has been Taiwan’s steadfast friend. We have weathered through many challenges together. In this 26th year of diplomatic relation, we are bound to have more and closer cooperation, and address regional and global challenges together. Finally, I sincerely thank every friend for attending the dinner tonight. I wish everyone continued health and success. Thank you! Mesulang!
